location.description: The location of the spark application.

serviceinstance.description: The id of the service instance to which this spark application belongs.

main_python_file_uri.description: The HCFS URI of the main Python file to use as the driver. Must be a .py file.

args.description: The arguments to pass to the driver.  Do not include arguments, such as `--conf`, that can be set as job properties, since a collision may occur that causes an incorrect job submission.

python_file_uris.description: HCFS file URIs of Python files to pass to the PySpark framework. Supported file types: .py, .egg, and .zip.

jar_file_uris.description: HCFS URIs of jar files to add to the CLASSPATHs of the Python driver and tasks.

file_uris.description: HCFS URIs of files to be placed in the working directory of each executor. Useful for naively parallel tasks.

archive_uris.description: HCFS URIs of archives to be extracted into the working directory of each executor. Supported file types: .jar, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, and .zip.

spark_application_config.description: Represents the SparkApplicationConfig. Structure is [documented below](#nested_spark_application_config).

main_jar_file_uri.description: The HCFS URI of the jar file that contains the main class.

main_class.description: The name of the driver main class. The jar file that contains the class must be in the classpath or specified in `jar_file_uris`.

args.description: The arguments to pass to the driver. Do not include arguments that can be set as application properties, such as `--conf`, since a collision can occur that causes an incorrect application submission.

jar_file_uris.description: HCFS URIs of jar files to add to the classpath of the Spark driver and tasks.

file_uris.description: HCFS URIs of files to be placed in the working directory of each executor.

archive_uris.description: HCFS URIs of archives to be extracted into the working directory of each executor. Supported file types: `.jar`, `.tar`, `.tar.gz`, `.tgz`, and `.zip`.

main_r_file_uri.description: The HCFS URI of the main R file to use as the driver. Must be a .R file.

args.description: The arguments to pass to the driver.  Do not include arguments, such as `--conf`, that can be set as job properties, since a collision may occur that causes an incorrect job submission.

file_uris.description: HCFS URIs of files to be placed in the working directory of each executor. Useful for naively parallel tasks.

archive_uris.description: HCFS URIs of archives to be extracted into the working directory of each executor. Supported file types: .jar, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, and .zip.

query_file_uri.description: The HCFS URI of the script that contains SQL queries.

query_list.description: Represents a list of queries. Structure is [documented below](#nested_spark_sql_application_config_query_list).

script_variables.description: Mapping of query variable names to values (equivalent to the Spark SQL command: SET `name="value";`).

jar_file_uris.description: HCFS URIs of jar files to be added to the Spark CLASSPATH.

display_name.description: User-provided human-readable name to be used in user interfaces.

labels.description: The labels to associate with this application. Labels may be used for filtering and billing tracking. **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration. Please refer to the field `effective_labels` for all of the labels present on the resource.

annotations.description: The annotations to associate with this application. Annotations may be used to store client information, but are not used by the server. **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the annotations present in your configuration. Please refer to the field `effective_annotations` for all of the annotations present on the resource.

version.description: The Dataproc version of this application.

application_environment.description: An ApplicationEnvironment from which to inherit configuration properties.

namespace.description: The Kubernetes namespace in which to create the application. This namespace must already exist on the cluster.

dependency_images.description: List of container image uris for additional file dependencies. Dependent files are sequentially copied from each image. If a file with the same name exists in 2 images then the file from later image is used.

project.description: If it is not provided, the provider project is used.
